podcasts.name: Davie Fogarty
podcasts.title: Why Most People Never Become Who They Want to Be
podcasts.transcript: At the end of the day, we are what we continually do. Our thoughts impact our beliefs, which impact our actions. This will then change our identity. Let's say you want to be a writer. Eventually, you'll stop thinking, "I'm bad at writing." You'll start thinking, "I am a writer, so I can do this." Let's say you're struggling to put down the vape, the cigarette, you'll say, "I'm not a smoker." This self-identity will get reinforced through beliefs and through actions over time. But, here's the key. Generally, the old you will always creep back in. There will always be behavioral, environmental things, or deep down subconscious things that will constantly push you back to your old habits. >> [music] >> And you just need to constantly remind yourself and push through. The truth is, it's very easy to get whatever you want. All you need to do is be willing to struggle through the friction. Now, the friction is that one moment when you are trying to do something and a thought pops into your head and you're like, "I'm not going to [music] do it." It's when you're running 10K's and you're at the 8K mark and you go, "I'm not good at doing it." It's when you go to start writing the first chapter of your book and it's like, "Oh, maybe the idea isn't good." That is the friction. You'll start noticing these moments. And the people that can push through that and continually grow that tolerance of pain through that can get anything that they want.
